title: The Dark Knight
logline: When a psychotic criminal known as the Joker plunges Gotham into chaos, Batman must confront the limits of his vigilantism while district attorney Harvey Dent wages war on organized crime, forcing all three men toward a collision that will determine the soul of the city.
antagonistOrConflict: The Joker and the moral chaos he represents
tone: Dark, operatic, morally complex with bursts of visceral action and psychological tension
themes: The cost of heroism and sacrifice
The thin line between hero and vigilante

actBreaks.description: Batman, Dent, and Gordon form their alliance on the MCU rooftop, committing to take down the mob together and setting the central conflict in motion.

validation.ruling: Scene #54 (p30, 21%) ends Act 1 as Batman, Dent, and Gordon form their alliance and commit to going after Lau and the mob's money despite knowing things will get ugly—this irreversible decision fundamentally changes the story's direction, raises stakes, and forces all three into new, higher-stakes conflict they cannot retreat from.

actBreaks.description: The Joker escapes custody after his interrogation and the subsequent explosion at MCU, having successfully turned the tables and put both Rachel and Dent in mortal danger.

validation.ruling: Scene #243 (p94, 67%) ends Act 2 as the medic discovers the phone implanted in the thug's body—this is the final moment before the explosion that allows the Joker to escape, representing the catastrophic failure of the heroes' plans and the irreversible shift into the darkest consequences where Rachel dies and Dent becomes Two-Face.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality:
This is a tightly-wound crime epic that operates like a pressure cooker, escalating relentlessly from a bank heist to citywide moral collapse. The script employs a three-act structure but subverts expectations by making Act Two catastrophically long (scenes 55-243, roughly 90 pages), which mirrors the Joker's strategy of exhausting his opponents. The first act efficiently establishes the status quo and the alliance between Batman, Dent, and Gordon. The massive second act becomes a war of attrition where every victory (capturing Lau, the mob roundup) is immediately countered by the Joker's escalations (killing officials, hostage scenarios). The third act is surprisingly compact, focusing on the ferries gambit and the final confrontation with Two-Face, allowing the moral devastation to land without overstaying.

What makes this structure distinctive is how it weaponizes pacing against audience expectations. The Hong Kong sequence (scenes 62-81) feels like it should be a climax but arrives at the act one break. The convoy chase (scenes 158-215) is positioned where most films place their midpoint, but here it's the launch into the interrogation sequence that truly shifts the story's center of gravity. The script understands that the Joker's chaos isn't a single explosive event but a sustained campaign of psychological warfare.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing operates in waves of compression and expansion. The opening bank heist (scenes 1-19) is a masterclass in economical action storytelling, establishing the Joker's methodology in under 7 pages. The script then downshifts into procedural mode (scenes 20-54) as we meet the key players and establish the conspiracy. Post-Hong Kong, the pacing becomes relentless and fragmented—the script cuts between 4-6 simultaneous storylines during the parade assassination attempt (scenes 128-141) and the convoy sequence (scenes 158-215), creating a sense of overwhelming chaos. The interrogation sequence (scenes 221-255) is the eye of the hurricane: mostly two-handers in confined spaces, yet the cross-cutting to Rachel and Dent's separate locations maintains unbearable tension. The final act (scenes 311-388) alternates between the claustrophobic ferry interiors and the vertiginous Prewitt Building confrontation, before collapsing into the intimate tragedy of the warehouse finale.

validation.ruling: Scene #6 is merely the opening action sequence setup. Scene #49 (p26, 18%) is the true inciting incident where the Joker crashes the mob meeting, kills Gambol's men, and proposes to kill Batman for half their money—this shatters the status quo, establishes the main conflict, introduces irreversible stakes, and forces all parties into new action they cannot ignore.

midpoint.description: The Joker, bleeding from nose and mouth, laughs at Batman and taunts him about Harvey and his fiancée; Batman smashes him into the wall and punches him hard demanding to know where they are; the Joker reveals Batman must choose between saving the district attorney or his bride-to-be.
midpoint.sceneHeading: INT. INTERROGATION ROOM, MCU, GOTHAM CENTRAL -- NIGHT

validation.ruling: Scene #226 (p89, 63%) is a textbook midpoint where the Joker reveals Rachel and Dent's locations, forcing Batman into an impossible choice—this is the point of no return that shifts Batman from reactive investigation to proactive rescue, raises stakes to maximum personal danger, and represents a false victory (getting information) that's actually a trap.

climax.description: Dent holds Gordon's son at gunpoint and declares he will use chance to decide who lives and dies, then flips his coin for Gordon's son, Batman tackles Dent, and both fall over the edge.
climax.sceneHeading: INT. SECOND FLOOR, BURNT WAREHOUSE -- CONTINUOUS

validation.ruling: Scene #383 (p135, 96%) is the climactic confrontation where Batman desperately tries to reach the broken Dent who holds Gordon's son at gunpoint, debating morality and responsibility—this is the peak dramatic tension, the ultimate obstacle Batman must face, and where the main thematic question about heroism and sacrifice reaches its resolution through direct confrontation.

scenes.heading: EXT. UNDERPASS, GOTHAM -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A Chinese man finishes loading cash boxes into a tractor trailer, which pulls out into a convoy.

scenes.heading: INT. BANK VAULT -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon stands furious in an almost empty bank vault as Lau appears on screen explaining he couldn't wait for permission.

scenes.heading: INT. SOCIAL CLUB, DOWNTOWN -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Lau appears on TV assuring that the money is safe as he boards his private jet, and the Bat Signal appears in the sky.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROOF, POLICE STATION -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman meets Dent on the rooftop, and Gordon bursts in as Dent and Gordon argue about leaks in their offices regarding the failed vault raid.

scenes.heading: INT. FOX'S OFFICE, WAYNE INDUSTRIES -- DAY
scenes.summary: Fox tells Wayne that Lau left town before he could cancel their deal, and Wayne suggests Fox should go to Hong Kong.

scenes.heading: INT. PRIVATE ELEVATOR -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Wayne tells Fox that Lau deserves a more personal touch than a phone call as Fox turns a key in the private elevator.

scenes.heading: INT. APPLIED SCIENCES DIVISION -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Fox shows Wayne high-altitude jump equipment and a new Bat-suit with hardened kevlar plates on titanium-dipped fiber as Wayne asks about getting back into a plane without it landing.

scenes.heading: INT. BAT-BUNKER -- DAY
scenes.summary: Wayne examines a parachute harness as Alfred shows him a diagram of a Navy cargo plane and confirms he found one in Arizona and arranged South Korean smugglers for the flight crew.

scenes.heading: EXT. BALLET -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rachel and Dent arrive at the ballet to find it canceled with a newspaper story showing Bruce Wayne has absconded with the entire Moscow ballet on his yacht.

scenes.heading: EXT. DECK, WAYNE'S YACHT, THE CARIBBEAN -- DAY
scenes.summary: Alfred navigates sunbathing ballerinas on Wayne's yacht as Wayne tosses a kit bag into the water and jumps in, swimming toward a sea-plane.

scenes.heading: INT. POOL HALL -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gambol's bodyguards bring in three bounty hunters who claim to have killed the Joker and present a body wrapped in garbage bags, which Gambol examines.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Joker springs his trap on Gambol, revealing he's alive and turning the tables on the crime boss who put a bounty on him.

scenes.heading: EXT.PENINSULA HOTEL, HONG KONG -- DAY
scenes.summary: Fox arrives by helicopter at the Peninsula Hotel in Hong Kong and is greeted by L.S.I. VPs who explain Lau cannot meet him due to legal difficulties.

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY, L.S.I.HOLDINGS -- DAY
scenes.summary: Fox is asked to surrender his mobile phone for security reasons and hands it to a security guard who places it in a box.

scenes.heading: INT. L.S.I.HOLDINGS -- DAY
scenes.summary: Fox and Lau eat lunch as Lau apologizes for leaving Gotham, and Fox's second cell phone rings, which he turns off and places by his plate as Lau notes they don't allow cell phones.

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY, L.S.I.HOLDINGS -- DAY
scenes.summary: Fox walks through security and declines to take back his original phone, showing he has an identical one, while the VP returns the original to the tray.

scenes.heading: INT. HOLD, C-130 CARGO HAULER -- DAWN
scenes.summary: Wayne, in flight suit and balaclava, receives a signal from the copilot, pulls on his oxygen mask, and jumps from the rear of the cargo plane.

scenes.heading: EXT. SKIES ABOVE HONG KONG -- DAWN
scenes.summary: Wayne plummets toward Hong Kong harbor, speeding past skyscrapers seconds from impact before pulling his chute and dropping into the water.

scenes.heading: EXT. BENEATH FREEWAY, HONG KONG HARBOR -- DAY
scenes.summary: Wayne pulls himself out of the water beneath a freeway, dragging up his pack.

scenes.heading: EXT. CENTRAL ESCALATORS -- DAY
scenes.summary: Wayne meets Fox on a crowded escalator where Fox shows him a 3-D map of Lau's office suite on a phone that uses high frequencies to map environments.

scenes.heading: EXT. HONG KONG -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman assembles a rifle-like device on a Hong Kong rooftop and fires four sticky bombs with visible countdown timers onto a lower building's glass.

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY, L.S.I.HOLDINGS -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Fox's cell phone glows under the security guard's desk as the monitor flickers off, lights dim, and all security doors open at once.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROOFTOP OVERLOOKING L.S.I.HOLDINGS
scenes.summary: Batman launches from the tall tower, his pack bursting open into bat wings as he glides down and banks hard toward a window in the rear of the lower building.

scenes.heading: INT. LAU'S OFFICE, L.S.I.HOLDINGS -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Lau talks on the phone in his office when the room suddenly goes dark.

scenes.heading: EXT. L.S.I.HOLDINGS -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Batman hurtles toward the glass, collapses his wings, wraps his cape around himself, and cannonballs through the window.

scenes.heading: INT. LAU'S OFFICE SUITE, L.S.I.HOLDINGS -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman rolls across the floor in a flurry of broken glass inside Lau's office suite.

scenes.heading: INT. LAU'S OFFICE, TOP FLOOR, L.S.I.HOLDINGS -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Lau pulls out a handgun in his darkened office.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY, L.S.I.HOLDINGS -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Lau steps into the hallway where his bodyguards with flashlights are waiting, and he demands to know where the cops are as they head for the stairwell.

scenes.heading: EXT. L.S.I.HOLDINGS -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: A small army of Hong Kong police led by a detective descends on the L.S.I. building.

scenes.heading: INT. MEZZANINE LEVEL, L.S.I.HOLDINGS -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Lau and his men move onto the mezzanine where flashlights go dark one by one, someone screams, and Lau fires his weapon multiple times with muzzle flashes strobing the room.

scenes.heading: EXT. L.S.I.HOLDINGS, HONG KONG -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Cops swarm into the L.S.I. building as a lobby security guard directs the Hong Kong detective where to go.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE, L.S.I.HOLDINGS -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman tackles Lau and straps a small pack onto him as the sticky bombs explode, blowing out the wall and ceiling to reveal the dawn sky, and a C-130 cargo plane appears overhead.
scenes.turningPointNote: Batman successfully extracts Lau from Hong Kong, securing the key witness needed to prosecute Gotham's mob.

scenes.heading: INT. GORDON'S OFFICE, MCU, GOTHAM CENTRAL -- DAY
scenes.summary: Ramirez walks into Gordon's office and tells him he's going to want to see something.

scenes.heading: EXT. MCU -- DAY
scenes.summary: Gordon follows Ramirez through excited cops to find Lau trussed on the ground with a sign taped to his chest reading 'Please deliver to Lieutenant Gordon.'

scenes.heading: INT. INTERROGATION ROOM, MCU, GOTHAM CENTRAL -- DAY
scenes.summary: Rachel offers Lau a deal for the money, but Lau refuses and instead offers to give up his clients, the mob's bosses, in exchange for immunity, protection, and a chartered plane back to Hong Kong.
scenes.turningPointNote: Lau agrees to testify against the mob bosses, giving Dent the leverage he needs for a massive RICO case.

scenes.heading: INT. OBSERVATION ROOM, MCU, GOTHAM CENTRAL -- DAY
scenes.summary: Dent tells Gordon and Rachel he can use RICO to charge all the mob members as one criminal conspiracy if they can charge any conspirator with a felony.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERROGATION ROOM, MCU, GOTHAM CENTRAL -- DAY
scenes.summary: Rachel asks Lau about the communal fund's details, and Lau demands immunity, protection, and a chartered plane in exchange for testifying in open court.

scenes.heading: INT. OBSERVATION ROOM, MCU, GOTHAM CENTRAL -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon insists on keeping Lau in the MCU holding cells rather than County, and Dent tells Gordon it's his call but warns him to be right.

scenes.heading: EXT. CITY HALL -- DAY
scenes.summary: Dent tells reporters at a press conference that he doesn't know about Lau's travel arrangements but is glad he's back.

scenes.heading: INT. RESTAURANT -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Chechen tells Maroni they should hire the Joker to fix the real problem, Batman, as Gordon arrives with handcuffs and points to a prison bus outside.

scenes.heading: EXT. VARIOUS DOWNTOWN AND OUTLYING LOCATIONS -- DAY
scenes.summary: Prison buses load criminals in every neighborhood across Gotham as Stephens puts a crime boss into one.

scenes.heading: INT. COURTROOM A, GOTHAM MUNICIPAL COURTHOUSE -- DAY
scenes.summary: Judge Surrillo reads a massive list of charges to the defendants, finds a Joker playing card on her page, and asks how they plead as defense lawyers yell all at once.

scenes.heading: INT. MAYOR'S OFFICE -- DAY
scenes.summary: The Mayor and Commissioner Loeb confront Dent about arresting 549 criminals at once, but Dent confidently defends his strategy and tells them to watch him.

scenes.heading: EXT. CITY HALL -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Pedestrians scream and point at Batman hanging dead by his neck from a flagpole five stories up at City Hall, his mouth painted in a demonic clown smile.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Joker escalates his campaign by publicly displaying a fake Batman corpse with a threatening message, directly challenging the real Batman.

scenes.heading: EXT. CITY HALL -- MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: The fake Batman is lowered from the flagpole, revealed to be the same vigilante from earlier, with a Joker card pinned to his chest reading 'WILL THE REAL BATMAN PLEASE STAND UP?'

scenes.heading: INT. WAYNE PENTHOUSE -- DAY
scenes.summary: Wayne sees news footage of the fake Batman hanging and watches a disturbing video of a blindfolded man in a Batman costume being tortured and forced to read a statement before being killed.

scenes.heading: INT. WAYNE PENTHOUSE -- EVENING
scenes.summary: Dent arrives at Wayne's fundraiser and talks with Alfred, who deflects Dent's question about Rachel's psychotic ex-boyfriends.

scenes.heading: EXT. HELIPAD, WAYNE PENTHOUSE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Wayne's helicopter touches down on the penthouse helipad and he spills out with a clutch of supermodels.

scenes.heading: INT. WAYNE PENTHOUSE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Wayne arrives late with supermodels, makes a speech mocking then praising Dent, and raises a toast declaring he believes in Harvey Dent.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR, MCU -- EVENING
scenes.summary: Ramirez tells Gordon that forensics found DNA on the Joker card belonging to Judge Surrillo, Harvey Dent, and Commissioner Loeb, and Gordon orders protective custody and seals City Hall.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Joker reveals his specific targets through DNA evidence, forcing Gordon to scramble to protect the key figures in the mob prosecution.

scenes.heading: EXT. DECK, WAYNE'S PENTHOUSE -- EVENING
scenes.summary: Wayne tells Rachel that the day Gotham no longer needs Batman is coming, and Rachel says she can't wait for that as Wayne insists it's happening now with Harvey.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET, GOTHAM HEIGHTS -- EVENING
scenes.summary: Two men in suits present badges to Judge Surrillo at her brownstone door.

scenes.heading: EXT. CITY HALL -- EVENING
scenes.summary: Gordon enters City Hall through tight police security.

scenes.heading: INT. POLICE COMMISSIONER'S OFFICE -- EVENING
scenes.summary: Gordon tells Loeb the Joker has threatened his life and orders a building search, but Loeb dismisses the threat and pours himself whisky.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET, GOTHAM HEIGHTS -- EVENING
scenes.summary: The two men give Surrillo a sealed envelope and tell her to open it in the car; when she does, it reads 'UP' and her car explodes.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Joker's assassination campaign begins with Judge Surrillo's murder

scenes.heading: INT. POLICE COMMISSIONER'S OFFICE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon realizes something is wrong and spins around, but Loeb is already drinking the poisoned whisky.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN, WAYNE PENTHOUSE -- EVENING
scenes.summary: Dent pulls Rachel into the kitchen and tells her the mob threat makes him think about who he wants to spend the rest of his life with.

scenes.heading: INT.LIVING ROOM, PENTHOUSE -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Joker and his thugs burst into Wayne's penthouse, smashing Wuertz over the head and announcing they are the entertainment.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Joker invades Wayne's penthouse to find Harvey Dent

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN, WAYNE PENTHOUSE -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Wayne puts Dent in a sleeper hold, knocks him unconscious, stuffs him in a closet, and tells Rachel to stay hidden.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM, WAYNE'S PENTHOUSE -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Joker and his thugs pour into the living room with weapons raised.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY, WAYNE PENTHOUSE -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Wayne disarms a thug, snaps his forearm, smashes him with the shotgun stock, and field strips the weapon.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM, WAYNE'S PENTHOUSE -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Joker asks for Harvey Dent, threatens his loved ones, grabs a gentleman and puts a blade in his mouth until Rachel tells him to stop.

scenes.heading: INT. MASTER BEDROOM, WAYNE PENTHOUSE -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Wayne seals himself in a panic room, leaving a couple behind who protest being locked out.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM, WAYNE'S PENTHOUSE -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman confronts the Joker, they fight, Batman disarms multiple thugs and breaks forearms, but the Joker kicks a blade between Batman's armor plates and escapes with Rachel out the window.
scenes.turningPointNote: Batman and the Joker's first direct physical confrontation; the Joker takes Rachel hostage

scenes.heading: EXT. BUILDING -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman fires his grapple to snag Rachel's ankle mid-fall and they slam into a passing taxi.

scenes.heading: INT. TAXI -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The taxi driver screams as Batman and Rachel hit the roof, roll down the windshield, and land on the pavement alive.

scenes.heading: INT. CAR -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Joker looks back exhilarated as his car speeds away, touches his blood, and tells his driver he's a man of his word.

scenes.heading: EXT. TAXI -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rachel confirms she's alright and thanks Batman, who tells her Harvey is safe.

scenes.heading: INT. MAJOR CRIMES UNIT, GOTHAM CENTRAL -- DAY
scenes.summary: Stephens tells Gordon the prosecution is over because no one will stand before a judge, but Dent bursts in asking where they keep their trash.

scenes.heading: INT. SPECIAL HOLDING AREA, MCU, GOTHAM CENTRAL -- DAY
scenes.summary: Dent threatens Lau with county jail to force his cooperation on the stand.

scenes.heading: INT. BAT-BUNKER -- DAY
scenes.summary: Wayne analyzes the Joker's video and tells Alfred criminals aren't complicated, but Alfred warns him the Joker may be a man he doesn't understand and tells a story about a bandit in Burma who couldn't be bought and just wanted to watch the world burn.

scenes.heading: EXT. SKYLINE OF GOTHAM -- DAWN
scenes.summary: Batman listens from a rooftop as a voice on the radio reports Harvey Dent's location at 8th and Orchard.

scenes.heading: EXT. 8TH STREET AT ORCHARD -- DAWN
scenes.summary: Gordon and Ramirez arrive at 8th and Orchard and lead officers into the building.

scenes.heading: INT. APARTMENT, EIGHT AVE. -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon finds two dead men with joker cards and crude smiles carved into their faces, their licenses reading Patrick Harvey and Richard Dent, and Batman asks for ten minutes with the scene.

scenes.heading: INT. FOX'S OFFICE, WAYNE ENTERPRISES -- DAY
scenes.summary: Reese shows Fox a blueprint revealing he knows Batman's identity and threatens to go to the press unless Fox reconsiders his position.

scenes.heading: INT. BAT-BUNKER -- DAY
scenes.summary: Wayne fires a computer-controlled gatling gun at brick wall samples, then uses an X-ray scanner to create a 3-D image of bullet fragments.

scenes.heading: INT. APPLIED SCIENCES DIVISION -- DAY
scenes.summary: Fox reverse-engineers the bullet fragments to reveal a thumbprint, and Wayne asks him to cross-reference addresses near Parkside.

scenes.heading: INT. BAT-BUNKER -- CONTINUOUS -- INTERCUT
scenes.summary: Alfred finds a match at 1502 Randolph Apartments overlooking the parade, and Wayne takes his motorcycle up on the lift.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARKSIDE AVENUE -- DAY
scenes.summary: Police march in the memorial parade as Engel reports on the Joker's threat to kill the Mayor, while snipers scan from above.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM STREETS -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Wayne maneuvers his motorcycle through traffic, parks near a barricade, and slips into an alley.

scenes.heading: INT. TENEMENT -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Wayne counts doors down a tenement hallway and finds apartment 1502.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARKSIDE AVENUE -- LATER
scenes.summary: The Mayor speaks at the memorial ceremony with Gordon, Dent, and Rachel in attendance.

scenes.heading: INT. TENEMENT -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Wayne finds eight bound and blindfolded men who tell him their guns and uniforms were taken.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARKSIDE AVENUE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon scans the crowd as the Mayor finishes and the Honor Guard raises weapons.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROOFTOP OVERLOOKING PARKSIDE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A police sniper scans the windows of the tenement.

scenes.heading: INT. TENEMENT -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Wayne races to the window and looks through the scope.

scenes.heading: EXT. STATE STREET -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Honor Guard turns their weapons on the Mayor, revealing one is the Joker, and Gordon leaps forward taking shots to the back as he slams the Mayor down.
scenes.turningPointNote: Gordon appears to be killed saving the Mayor from the Joker's assassination attempt

scenes.heading: EXT. ROOFTOP OVERLOOKING PARKSIDE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The police sniper spots Wayne at the window and shoots.

scenes.heading: INT. TENEMENT -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Wayne ducks as shots erupt around the window.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARKSIDE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Pandemonium erupts, one honor guard is shot in the leg, the others melt into chaos, and Stephens rolls Gordon over to find he is not moving.

scenes.heading: EXT. SIDE STREET OFF PARKSIDE -- MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Dent approaches an ambulance in the chaos and steps into the back.

scenes.heading: INT. AMBULANCE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Dent finds the Joker's handcuffed thug wearing a name tag reading 'Officer Rachel Dawes,' spots the keys in the ignition, and jumps into the driver's seat.

scenes.heading: EXT. GORDON'S HOUSE -- DUSK
scenes.summary: Stephens tells Barbara Gordon that her husband is dead, she blames Batman who watches from the shadows, and she collapses sobbing.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROOF, MCU -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Stephens tells detectives to switch off the bat-signal because Batman isn't coming and doesn't want to talk to them.

scenes.heading: INT. NIGHTCLUB -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman savages Maroni's bodyguards in a nightclub and lands on the table in front of him.

scenes.heading: INT. MCU -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rachel calls Harvey asking where he is, and he warns her she's not safe at Gordon's unit because the Joker has named her next.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT, CONDEMNED BUILDING -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Dent tells Rachel he loves her and hangs up, revealing he has the Joker's thug taped to a chair and blindfolded in a basement.

scenes.heading: EXT. FIRE ESCAPE -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman holds Maroni by the collar off a fire escape and demands the Joker's location, then drops him when Maroni says the fall won't kill him.

scenes.heading: EXT. SIDEWALK -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Maroni hits the pavement with a broken leg, and Batman tells him someone knows where the Joker is, but Maroni says no one will cross the Joker and Batman has only one way to get him.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT, CONDEMNED BUILDING -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Dent threatens the Joker's thug with a gun and his lucky coin, flipping it to decide the thug's fate, and the terrified thug finally reveals the Joker's location at 250 52nd Street.
scenes.turningPointNote: Dent crosses a moral line using torture and his coin to extract information; obtains the Joker's location

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM, WAYNE PENTHOUSE -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rachel tells Wayne that Harvey says Batman will turn himself in, and Wayne says he has no choice and has seen what he would have to become, then asks if they can be together now, but Rachel tells him not to make her his one hope for a normal life.

scenes.heading: INT. BAT-BUNKER -- DAWN
scenes.summary: Alfred burns documents and blueprints in an incinerator while Wayne orders him to destroy anything connecting to Lucius or Rachel, and Alfred tells Wayne he must endure being hated because Batman can make the choice no one else can face.

scenes.heading: INT. PRESS ROOM, SUPERIOR COURT -- DAY
scenes.summary: Dent holds a press conference announcing Batman has offered to turn himself in, but argues they shouldn't give in to the Joker's terrorist demands while Wayne sits in the crowd watching.

scenes.heading: INT. WAYNE PENTHOUSE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rachel watches Dent's press conference on television at Wayne's penthouse.

scenes.heading: INT. PRESS ROOM, SUPERIOR COURT -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The crowd at the press conference chants angrily for Batman while Dent delivers an impassioned speech promising the dawn is coming, then Dent loses control of the crowd.

scenes.heading: INT. WAYNE PENTHOUSE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rachel stares appalled at the television as Dent is arrested.

scenes.heading: INT. WAYNE PENTHOUSE -- DAY
scenes.summary: Rachel confronts Alfred about why Bruce let Harvey take the fall, and Alfred explains Batman is making a sacrifice to be something more than a hero, but Rachel calls it unheroic and holds something out.

scenes.heading: INT. CELL, MCU -- EVENING
scenes.summary: Rachel visits Dent in his cell where he explains he's offering himself as bait so Batman can take down the Joker during the transfer, but Rachel protests it's too dangerous.

scenes.heading: EXT. COURTYARD MCU -- EVENING
scenes.summary: Dent is led shackled to the convoy while Rachel follows, warning him Batman is using him as bait and has failed so far, then Dent kisses her and flips his lucky coin before being loaded into the vehicle.
scenes.turningPointNote: Dent commits to the dangerous convoy plan that will trigger the Joker's major attack sequence

scenes.heading: INT. ARMORED CAR -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A SWAT officer with a shotgun climbs into the armored car cab and puts on his mask, then comments to the already-masked driver about hoping he has moves.

scenes.heading: EXT. TENTH AVENUE, DOWNTOWN -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The convoy rockets past a roadblock.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMORED CAR -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The SWAT officers inside the armored car stare at Dent, who smiles back at them.

scenes.heading: EXT. INTERSECTION -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: An officer holding up traffic is shot with a shotgun blast by the Joker in a truck.

scenes.heading: INT. LEAD PATROL CAR, CONVOY -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The SWAT driver in the lead patrol car slows as he sees something burning in the intersection ahead.

scenes.heading: EXT. AVENUE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A police helicopter hovers above a burning fire truck blocking the road.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMORED CAR -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The driver listens to a radio order redirecting all units to exit down Cheviot west and proceed north on lower 5th avenue, and the shotgun SWAT warns they'll be like ducks in a barrel down there.

scenes.heading: EXT. SURFACE STREETS -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The convoy disappears down the exit ramp.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOWER FIFTH AVENUE -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: A garbage truck pulls up behind the convoy and swipes the rear vehicles off the road.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMORED CAR -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The shotgun SWAT yells for the driver to get them out of there and the driver hits the gas.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOWER FIFTH -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The garbage truck pushes hard on the armored car, ramming its rear bumper and forcing it forward.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMORED CAR -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The driver watches the truck fill his rear view mirror while the shotgun SWAT picks up the radio to report company.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOWER FIFTH AVENUE -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: A second truck smashes the SWAT van into the river, then the Joker's truck pulls alongside the armored car and the Joker fires a machine gun at it while the garbage truck pushes it forward, bullets ricocheting off the armor.

scenes.heading: INT. REAR CABIN, ARMORED CAR -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Dent remains calm while the SWAT officers flinch from bullet indentations hitting the armored car.

scenes.heading: INT. UP FRONT, ARMORED CAR -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The shotgun SWAT stares at the Joker.

scenes.heading: INT. REAR TRAILER OF TRUCK -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Joker drops his machine gun and picks up an RPG, then stops and stares fascinated as he sees the Batmobile racing toward the second truck.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOWER FIFTH -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Batmobile plows straight into the second truck, sending it up into the concrete ceiling where it disintegrates as the Batmobile continues through.

scenes.heading: INT. TRUCK -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Joker looks back at the Batmobile amused, tells his thug anyone could be driving it and to stay on Dent, then lines up his RPG to fire.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMORED CAR -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The shotgun SWAT's eyes go wide at the Joker's RPG and the driver jams the brakes.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOWER FIFTH -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The armored car crashes back against the garbage truck and slows just enough that the RPG slams into the squad car in front instead, which explodes as the armored car bursts through the fireball.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOWER FIFTH -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Batmobile spins around to rejoin the pursuit.

scenes.heading: INT. JOKER'S TRUCK -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Joker turns to his men to reload his RPG, then turns back and levels it out the window to aim.

scenes.heading: INT. BATMOBILE -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman watches the Joker prepare to fire and toggles the afterburner.

scenes.heading: INT. PASSENGER CAR -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Two small children in the back of a passenger car watch as the Batmobile rockets overhead.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOWER LEVEL STREET -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Joker fires the RPG and the Batmobile crashes down between the vehicles, taking the hit which explodes and sends the flaming car spinning.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMORED CAR -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The SWAT officers react to the explosion while Dent remains calm.

scenes.heading: INT. CAB OF JOKER'S TRUCK -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The driver of the Joker's truck takes a debris hit to the head.

scenes.heading: INT. TRUCK -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Joker cackles with delight as he is thrown around the rear of the trailer.

scenes.heading: INT. BATMOBILE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Batman wrestles the pod controls, spinning on the gyro.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOWER LEVEL STREET -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Batmobile flips over and comes to rest as a smoking heap with the front end intact but rear wheels scattered, and a small crowd gathers.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOWER LEVEL STREET -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Joker jumps down from the truck giggling, looks back at the Batmobile's destruction and says whoever he was he isn't now, then yanks his dead driver out and takes the wheel to pull back onto the roadway.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOWER LEVEL STREET -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The armored car pulls ahead with the Joker's truck in hot pursuit.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMORED CAR -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The shotgun SWAT glances in the rear view mirror, slaps the dash, and tells the driver to get topside for air support.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOWER LEVEL STREET -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The armored car pulls onto a ramp heading up with the Joker's truck following.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOWER LEVEL STREET -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: People stare at the smoking wreck and inch closer.

scenes.heading: INT. BATMOBILE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Batman adjusts his position and hits a button triggering the Batmobile's eject and self-destruct sequence as arm guards grab his forearms and explosive bolts fire around the pod.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOWER LEVEL STREET -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Batman emerges from the flaming Batmobile hoisted up by the front pod which transforms into a motorcycle-type vehicle called the Bat-Pod, then shoots forward as the Batmobile detonates in a massive fireball.
scenes.turningPointNote: Batman's primary vehicle is destroyed but he emerges with a new tactical advantage, transforming the chase dynamic

scenes.heading: EXT. PARKSIDE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The armored car races down the street as the police chopper dips low and the pilot reports they're back on point and ready to give some of their own medicine.

scenes.heading: INT. POLICE CHOPPER -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A cop in the police chopper pulls out an assault rifle and cocks it.

scenes.heading: INT. CAB OF JOKER'S TRUCK -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Joker fights with the truck's gearbox, then turns to his thug and tells him to tee them up, and the thug grabs his radio.

scenes.heading: EXT. FIRE ESCAPE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A Joker thug in a clown mask on a fire escape loads up a cable gun.

scenes.heading: EXT. SECOND FIRE ESCAPE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Another thug on a second fire escape loads his own cable gun.

scenes.heading: INT. PASSENGER CAR, LOWER LEVEL STREET -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: A motorist watches through his rear-view mirror as the bat-pod tears past and smashes off his wing mirror.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOWER LEVEL STREET -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman accelerates the bat-pod through traffic, cutting across intersections and blasting parked cars out of the way to create a path.

scenes.heading: EXT. TRAIN STATION -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The bat-pod crashes through glass doors into a train station, races through as commuters scream and dive away, then speeds up stairs to the upper street.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARKSIDE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Joker's thugs fire cables across the street that catch the police chopper, bringing it down in a fiery crash that barrels toward the armored car.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMORED CAR -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The driver steers the armored car around the flaming helicopter wreckage as the shotgun SWAT shuts his eyes.

scenes.heading: EXT. NARROW ALLEY -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The bat-pod tears down a narrow alley blocked with dumpsters and blasts them aside to make a path.

scenes.heading: INT. CAB OF JOKER'S TRUCK -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Joker hands the wheel to his thug and takes a submachine gun as the bat-pod emerges from an alley and races toward them.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARKSIDE -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The bat-pod races straight at the Joker's truck and fires a harpoon that impacts below the bumper.

scenes.heading: INT. TRUCK -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Joker's thug ducks from the harpoon shot, then comes back up beaming and says the shot missed.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARKSIDE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Batman swerves past the truck, wrapping cables around a lamp post and spinning to a halt to watch.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARKSIDE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The cable goes taut and flips the truck end over end; the Joker crawls from the wreckage and waves his pistol at oncoming traffic as Batman guns the bat-pod over the median.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMORED CAR -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The driver slows the armored car and pulls to the side as the shotgun SWAT asks what he's doing.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARKSIDE -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Joker walks toward the speeding bat-pod firing randomly at traffic and daring Batman to hit him; Batman locks up the brakes and dumps the bike rather than hit him, slamming into a wall unconscious; the Joker's thugs reach Batman first but are shocked when trying to remove the mask, then the Joker approaches with a switchblade.
scenes.turningPointNote: Batman chooses not to kill the Joker and crashes instead, leaving himself vulnerable and unconscious — a critical moment showing his moral line and the Joker's psychological victory.

scenes.heading: INT. ARMORED CAR -- MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Gordon opens the armored car door to reveal himself alive to Dent, who grins in relief and respect as they shake hands.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARKSIDE -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Cops seal off the roadway as Gordon drives away with the Joker; reporters clamor around Dent as Ramirez pushes them aside and escorts him to a squad car driven by Wuertz.

scenes.heading: INT. HOLDING, MCU, GOTHAM CENTRAL -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Joker sits calmly in a holding cage as cops smash nightsticks near his head without making him flinch; Gordon orders them to stand away and handle him carefully; the Mayor arrives and Gordon reports they have no matches on prints, DNA, or prior arrests.

scenes.heading: EXT. GORDON HOME -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gordon rings the bell at his home and Barbara, dressed in black, answers and slaps him before he grabs and holds her as she sobs.

scenes.heading: INT. HOLDING, MCU, GOTHAM CENTRAL -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Joker's men are processed in harsh light looking ridiculous in clown makeup; a fat thug complains he doesn't feel good and Detective Murphy tells him he's lucky to feel anything; the Joker smiles at this from his cage.

scenes.heading: INT. KIDS' BEDROOM, GORDON HOME -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gordon crouches by his son's bedside and touches his cheek; James Jr. wakes and whispers asking if Batman saved him, and Gordon says he saved Batman this time before his phone rings.

scenes.heading: INT. MAJOR CRIMES UNIT, GOTHAM CENTRAL -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gordon pushes through detectives crowded in the observation room watching the Joker on a monitor and asks if he's said anything; Ramirez shakes her head and Gordon pushes through a door.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERROGATION, MCU, GOTHAM CENTRAL -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gordon interrogates the Joker about Harvey Dent's whereabouts; the Joker taunts him about being alone and suggests Gordon's own people may have betrayed Dent, then cryptically says Dent might be in one spot or several depending on the time; Gordon undoes the handcuffs and leaves to get a cup of coffee.

scenes.heading: INT. OBSERVATION ROOM, MCU, GOTHAM CENTRAL -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: A detective moves for the door but Gordon stops him, saying the Joker is in control.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERROGATION ROOM, MCU, GOTHAM CENTRAL -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman hoists the Joker up by the neck demanding to know where Dent is; the Joker says Batman will have to break his one rule to know the truth and taunts that he'll have to choose between saving one of two people.

scenes.heading: INT. OBSERVATION ROOM, MCU, GOTHAM CENTRAL -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gordon moves for the door in the observation room.

scenes.heading: INT. OBSERVATION ROOM, MCU -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Batman jams a chair under the doorknob, picks up the Joker, and hurls him into the two-way glass, which spiders.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERROGATION ROOM, MCU, GOTHAM CENTRAL -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Joker, bleeding from nose and mouth, laughs at Batman and taunts him about Harvey and his fiancée; Batman smashes him into the wall and punches him hard demanding to know where they are; the Joker reveals Batman must choose between saving the district attorney or his bride-to-be.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Joker reveals the impossible choice — Batman must choose between saving Harvey Dent or Rachel, forcing him into the moral dilemma the Joker has orchestrated.

scenes.heading: INT. OBSERVATION ROOM, MCU, GOTHAM CENTRAL -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman races past Gordon, who asks which one he's going after; Batman says Dent knew the risks; Gordon looks back to see the Joker bloody but grinning.

scenes.heading: EXT. MCU, GOTHAM CENTRAL -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Several cops see Batman climb onto the bat-pod and tear off from MCU.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREETS, GOTHAM -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman swerves into oncoming traffic with chaos in his wake.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM CENTRAL -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gordon and his men scramble into their cars at Gotham Central.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT APARTMENT -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Harvey Dent wakes bound to a chair in a basement apartment and hears Rachel's voice through a speakerphone; he reassures her everything will be fine as he sees metal barrels hooked to a car battery with a timer counting down from five minutes.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERROGATION ROOM, MCU, GOTHAM CENTRAL -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Joker sits smiling and asks for his phone call; Stephens refuses and says the Joker killed six of his friends, distinguishing him as a freak who would just enjoy punishment.

scenes.heading: INT. HOLDING AREA, MCU, GOTHAM CENTRAL -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The fat thug shuffles to the bars complaining his insides hurt and collapses; the cop grabs his radio.

scenes.heading: INT. WAREHOUSE -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rachel, bound to a chair with barrels behind her, tells Harvey they don't have much time as the timer reads 2:47 and counting down.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT APARTMENT -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Dent drags his chair toward the barrels but it jams; Rachel says only one of them will make it and their friends will choose; Dent strains, topples over knocking over a barrel that spews diesel fuel around him as he continues trying to reach the timer.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREETS, GOTHAM -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The bat-pod skids sideways with wheels flipping, comes to rest, blows a fire exit door off its hinges, and Batman jumps off the bike.

scenes.heading: EXT. 52ND STREET, GOTHAM -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gordon swerves onto the sidewalk in gridlock traffic as people scatter.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERROGATION ROOM, MCU, GOTHAM CENTRAL -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Joker taunts Stephens about using a knife to savor emotions and knowing his friends better in their last moments; Stephens rolls up his sleeves saying he'll enjoy punching the Joker and does so.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT APARTMENT -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Dent, half submerged in diesel fuel, is overwhelmed with emotion as Rachel says she wants him to know something; he tells her not to think like that and that they're coming for her.

scenes.heading: INT. WAREHOUSE -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rachel looks at the timer with ten seconds left and tells Harvey she doesn't want to live without him and her answer is yes.

scenes.heading: INT. HOLDING AREA, MCU, GOTHAM CENTRAL -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: A medic cuts away the fat thug's shirt revealing a crude incision with a rectangular shape visible under the skin above his navel.

scenes.heading: INT. DETECTIVE'S ROOM, MCU, GOTHAM CENTRAL -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Stephens shuffles out with broken glass held to his throat by the Joker as cops draw weapons; the Joker demands his phone call and a detective tosses him a cell phone which he begins to dial.

scenes.heading: INT. HOLDING AREA, MCU, GOTHAM CENTRAL -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The medic presses the rectangle under the fat thug's skin and it illuminates with soft blue light as a cop asks if it's a phone.

scenes.heading: INT. DETECTIVE'S ROOM, MCU, GOTHAM CENTRAL -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Joker presses send and the door to the holding area explodes.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Joker triggers the bomb he planted inside his thug, creating an explosion that allows his escape and demonstrates his ruthless planning.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY, TENEMENT -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman sprints down a hallway, stops at a door, and kicks it.

scenes.heading: EXT. 52ND STREET, GOTHAM -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Cars pull up and Gordon gets out carrying a fire ax.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY, TENEMENT -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman kicks the door until it gives and smashes it open.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT APARTMENT -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman bursts through the door and Dent looks up in horror asking why Batman came for him instead of Rachel; Batman stares realizing the Joker lied as the counter hits five seconds; Batman drags Dent out as Dent fights to stay and screams for Rachel.
scenes.turningPointNote: Batman realizes the Joker gave him the wrong address — he's saving Dent when he intended to save Rachel, the irreversible consequence of the Joker's deception now in motion.

scenes.heading: EXT. 52ND STREET, GOTHAM -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gordon runs toward the entrance with an axe in hand.

scenes.heading: INT. WAREHOUSE -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rachel hears Dent's voice, remains calm, and begins to say something as the counter runs out and an all-consuming explosion occurs.
scenes.turningPointNote: Rachel dies in the explosion — an irreversible loss that will transform both Bruce and Harvey, the central tragedy of the film's second act.

scenes.heading: EXT. 52ND STREET, GOTHAM -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: An enormous explosion at the warehouse hurls Gordon backward onto the hood of his car.

scenes.heading: INT. BASEMENT APARTMENT -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman wraps his cape around Dent and hurls them both through the door as Dent screams.

scenes.heading: EXT. 52ND STREET, GOTHAM -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gordon picks himself up and tries to head toward the burning warehouse but five of his men restrain him.

scenes.heading: EXT. ALLEYWAY, GOTHAM -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: A second explosion hits Batman and Dent, igniting the diesel on Dent's left side; Batman rolls him on the pavement to extinguish the flames as Dent falls silent.
scenes.turningPointNote: Harvey Dent is severely burned and disfigured, transforming him physically and setting up his evolution into Two-Face

scenes.heading: INT. SPECIAL HOLDING AREA, MCU, GOTHAM CENTRAL -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Joker walks to the bars of another cell and greets the terrified Lau.

scenes.heading: EXT. AMBULANCE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Dent is wheeled into an ambulance with bandages held to his face, his one visible eye staring blankly.

scenes.heading: EXT. 52ND STREET -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon examines singed Joker cards with Lau's photo on them and learns from a sergeant that Dent is alive but the Joker has escaped with Lau; Gordon realizes the Joker planned to be caught.
scenes.turningPointNote: Gordon realizes the Joker orchestrated his own capture as part of a larger plan, revealing the depth of the villain's strategy

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET, DOWNTOWN GOTHAM -- DAWN
scenes.summary: The Joker sticks his head out of a speeding squad car window like a dog, feeling the wind.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN, WAYNE PENTHOUSE -- DAWN
scenes.summary: Alfred sits at a table reading Rachel's letter to Bruce.

scenes.heading: EXT. 52ND STREET -- DAWN
scenes.summary: Fire crews extinguish the last flames of the devastated building as a fireman points out something in the ruins.

scenes.heading: INT. INTENSIVE CARE UNIT -- DAWN
scenes.summary: Surgeons surround Dent in intensive care, working on his damaged face.

scenes.heading: EXT. 52ND STREET -- DAWN
scenes.summary: Firemen watch Batman standing statue-like amidst the charred ruins.

scenes.heading: INT. MCU, GOTHAM CENTRAL -- DAY
scenes.summary: Gordon surveys the wreckage and bodies at MCU.

scenes.heading: EXT. 52ND STREET -- DAWN
scenes.summary: Batman kneels in the smoldering debris and finds Dent's two-headed coin, now blackened and scarred on one side while the other side remains pristine.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN, WAYNE PENTHOUSE -- DAWN
scenes.summary: Alfred continues reading Rachel's letter.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL ROOM -- DAWN
scenes.summary: Batman stands at the foot of heavily bandaged Dent's hospital bed, apologizes to him, and places Dent's damaged coin on the bedside table.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN, WAYNE PENTHOUSE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Alfred finishes reading Rachel's letter with tears in his eyes, folds it back into its envelope, and places it on the breakfast tray.

scenes.heading: INT. WAYNE PENTHOUSE -- DAWN
scenes.summary: Alfred brings breakfast to Wayne, who questions whether he brought this madness on Rachel; Alfred insists Rachel believed in what they stand for and picks up the cowl, telling Wayne people are ready to believe in good.

scenes.heading: EXT. DOWNTOWN GOTHAM -- DAY
scenes.summary: A national guard helicopter roars over Gotham General hospital.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL ROOM -- DAY
scenes.summary: Dent wakes sedated, sees his coin on the table, marvels at its shiny face, then turns it over to see the devastated side and begins ripping his bandages.

scenes.heading: INT. WAYNE PENTHOUSE -- DAY
scenes.summary: Alfred shows Wayne a TV broadcast where Engel announces that Reese, a lawyer, will reveal Batman's true identity at five o'clock.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL ROOM -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon enters Dent's hospital room and apologizes about Rachel; Dent refuses medication and skin grafts, then angrily demands Gordon say his old Internal Affairs nickname, and when Gordon says 'Two-face,' Dent turns to reveal his destroyed left side.
scenes.turningPointNote: Harvey Dent fully embraces his Two-Face identity, marking his psychological transformation from hero to villain

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR, HOSPITAL -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Maroni on crutches tells Gordon the chaos is too much and offers to reveal where the Joker will be that afternoon.

scenes.heading: EXT. ABANDONED DOCKS -- DAY
scenes.summary: The Chechen and his bodyguards and dogs head up the gangplank of a rusted hulk at abandoned docks.

scenes.heading: INT. RUSTED HULK -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Chechen finds the Joker standing atop a billion-dollar pile of cash with bound Lau at his feet; the Joker grabs gasoline and tells the Chechen he enjoys dynamite and gasoline, then douses the money pile and Lau with gasoline.

scenes.heading: INT. TELEVISION STUDIO -- DAY
scenes.summary: Reese confidently defends his decision to reveal Batman's identity on Engel's show as callers question him and Engel mentions Harvey Dent's condition.

scenes.heading: INT. MAJOR CRIMES UNIT, GOTHAM CENTRAL -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon and his men gear up as they hear the Joker's broadcast threatening to blow up a hospital if Coleman Reese isn't dead in sixty minutes.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Joker escalates the stakes by threatening mass casualties, forcing the heroes to protect Reese while evacuating hospitals

scenes.heading: INT. WAYNE PENTHOUSE -- DAY
scenes.summary: Wayne tells Alfred to check Gordon's men and their families for hospital admissions, then heads out in the Lamborghini instead of the Batpod.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM STREETS -- DAY
scenes.summary: Wayne's Lamborghini tears through downtown Gotham.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Chaos erupts in the hospital as patients and staff run around while cops and traffic wardens manage the evacuation; two cops stationed outside Dent's room leave to help wheel gurneys.

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY, TELEVISION STUDIO -- DAY
scenes.summary: Gordon and his men escort Reese out of the television studio as an old man raises a pistol and fires, shattering the lobby glass; Gordon throws Reese to the ground and orders the cars brought around back.

scenes.heading: INT. LAMBORGHINI -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Wayne slows past the chaos outside the television station and reports seeing O'Brien and Richards.

scenes.heading: INT. BAT-BUNKER -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Alfred types at the computer and reports finding nothing on O'Brien and Richards regarding family members in hospitals.

scenes.heading: INT. STAIRWELL, TELEVISION STUDIO -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon pulls the shaken Reese down the stairs into a police van.

scenes.heading: INT. POLICE VAN -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon throws Reese in the back of the van and sarcastically suggests maybe Batman will save him as the van peels out.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOSPITAL -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cops load patients onto buses at the hospital; Polk radios Davis to bring Dent out but gets no answer and heads back toward the hospital.

scenes.heading: INT. LAMBORGHINI -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Wayne trails the police van and reports seeing Burns, Zachary, and an unknown patrolman to Alfred.

scenes.heading: INT. POLICE VAN -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon receives a text warning him to watch out for cops with relatives in Gotham hospitals, listing Burke, Ramirez, and Till.

scenes.heading: INT. DENT'S HOSPITAL ROOM -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Polk enters Dent's hospital room to find the Joker disguised as a redheaded nurse; the Joker turns and fires his silenced pistol at Polk.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM STREETS -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Lamborghini zips around a car to get closer to the police van.

scenes.heading: INT. POLICE VAN -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon reads the warning text and notices Berg nervously fingering his shotgun; Gordon asks if Berg is okay and Berg looks at his watch.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL ROOM -- DAY
scenes.summary: The Joker loosens Dent's restraints and tells him he's a dog chasing cars who doesn't have a plan, claiming the schemers put Dent where he is and that he just turned Dent's plan on itself.

scenes.heading: EXT. INTERSECTION, GOTHAM STREETS -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Wayne roars past traffic to settle in a few cars back from the police van at a red light.

scenes.heading: INT. LAMBORGHINI -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Wayne studies the intersection and spots a pickup jostling for position on the cross street.

scenes.heading: INT. POLICE VAN -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon watches Berg fidgeting with his shotgun and tells him he needs his weapon; Berg asks why.

scenes.heading: INT. LAMBORGHINI -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Wayne watches the pickup driver staring intently at the police van and lining it up.

scenes.heading: INT. POLICE VAN -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Berg's shotgun barrel inches lower as he trembles and asks Gordon if it's because his wife is in the hospital; Gordon confirms that's why.

scenes.heading: INT. DENT'S HOSPITAL ROOM -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Joker presses his head to Dent's gun barrel and tells him nobody panics when things go according to plan, but if he says the mayor will die tomorrow everyone loses their minds, because he's upset the established order and shown how pathetic their morals are.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Joker completes his philosophical corruption of Harvey Dent, convincing him that chaos and chance should rule rather than justice and order

scenes.heading: EXT. INTERSECTION, GOTHAM STREETS -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The light turns green and the police van pulls into the intersection as the pickup guns it and races toward the van.

scenes.heading: INT. LAMBORGHINI -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Wayne floors the Lamborghini and yanks the wheel to pull up onto the sidewalk.

scenes.heading: INT. POLICE VAN -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Berg nervously addresses Reese inside the moving police van.

scenes.heading: EXT. INTERSECTION, GOTHAM STREETS -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A pickup truck barrels toward the van but Wayne's Lamborghini slips alongside and takes the impact instead.

scenes.heading: INT. POLICE VAN -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon leaps forward and disarms Berg by pushing up his shotgun barrel and knocking him unconscious.

scenes.heading: EXT. INTERSECTION, GOTHAM STREETS -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon pulls Wayne from the wrecked Lamborghini and Wayne pretends he didn't know he was protecting the van carrying Reese.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR, HOSPITAL -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Joker walks calmly through the hospital corridor and pushes a detonator button, triggering staggered explosions behind him.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Joker destroys Gotham General Hospital, escalating chaos and demonstrating his control over the city.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOSPITAL -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Joker strolls down the hospital steps as windows blow out and the building collapses behind him while people dive for cover.

scenes.heading: EXT. INTERSECTION, GOTHAM STREETS -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon hears the explosion and calls to confirm whether Dent was evacuated from the hospital.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOSPITAL -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A cop cowers as debris and smoke blast across the street from the hospital explosion.

scenes.heading: INT. SCHOOL BUS -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Joker boards a school bus and nods at his driver.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOSPITAL -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: While everyone watches the collapsed building, one school bus pulls out from the line and drives away unnoticed.

scenes.heading: INT. BAR, GOTHAM HEIGHTS -- DAY
scenes.summary: Harvey Dent, hideously burned on the left side of his face, confronts Detective Wuertz at gunpoint in a bar and forces him to drink while interrogating him about who betrayed him.
scenes.turningPointNote: Harvey Dent is revealed to have survived but transformed into Two-Face, now seeking vengeance against those who betrayed him.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM GENERAL -- DAY
scenes.summary: Gordon frantically surveys the hospital scene and learns that Harvey Dent is missing while fifty people are unaccounted for.

scenes.heading: INT. FOX'S OFFICE WAYNE INDUSTRIES -- DAY
scenes.summary: Fox receives notice of a break-in at the Research and Development department while watching news of the hospital explosion.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR OUTSIDE RESEARCH AND DEVELOPMENT -- DAY
scenes.summary: Fox and security force open the R&D door and Fox enters alone.

scenes.heading: INT. LAB, RESEARCH AND DEVELOPMENT -- DAY
scenes.summary: Fox discovers Batman has turned every phone in Gotham into a sonar surveillance device, and Fox condemns it as unethical but Batman insists he needs it to find the Joker.

scenes.heading: EXT. SITUATION TENT AT GOTHAM GENERAL -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon watches the Joker's video message announcing his plan for nightfall.

scenes.heading: INT. BAR, DOWNTOWN -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Bar patrons watch the Joker's broadcast threatening that the city will be his at nightfall and warning the bridge-and-tunnel crowd of a surprise, then the feed cuts to static.

scenes.heading: INT. LAB, RESEARCH AND DEVELOPMENT -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Batman gives Fox an audio sample of the Joker's voice to triangulate his position using the citywide phone surveillance system, and Fox reluctantly agrees to help this one time.

scenes.heading: EXT. VARIOUS LOCATIONS -- DUSK
scenes.summary: Gotham citizens pour out of the city while bomb squads search deserted bridges and tunnels.

scenes.heading: INT. CITY HALL -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon briefs the Mayor on evacuation plans, proposing to use ferries to evacuate thirty thousand people and transport prisoners off the island.

scenes.heading: EXT. FERRY TERMINAL -- DUSK
scenes.summary: At the ferry terminal, civilians protest as eight hundred prisoners are loaded onto one ferry while they wait to board another.

scenes.heading: EXT. BROWNSTONE -- DUSK
scenes.summary: Maroni climbs into the back of a limo.

scenes.heading: INT. LIMO -- DUSK
scenes.summary: Two-Face confronts Maroni in the limo at gunpoint, threatening him about his wife and demanding to know who let the Joker off the leash besides Wuertz.

scenes.heading: EXT. BRIDGE -- DUSK
scenes.summary: The limo swerves off the bridge and crashes into the retaining wall.

scenes.heading: EXT. FERRY TERMINAL -- DUSK
scenes.summary: Civilians cram onto one ferry and both ferries depart for the mainland.

scenes.heading: INT. BRIDGE, PRISONER FERRY -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The pilot of the prisoner ferry notices the other ferry has lost engines, then his own control panel flickers and dies.

scenes.heading: INT. PASSENGER LOUNGE, PRISONER FERRY -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The First Mate moves through the prisoner ferry past prisoners and corrections officers.

scenes.heading: INT. ACCESS CORRIDOR, PRISONER FERRY -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The First Mate opens the engine room door and discovers hundreds of barrels of diesel fuel and a small wrapped present.

scenes.heading: INT. BRIDGE, PRISONER FERRY -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The pilot of the prisoner ferry holds the small present and receives a radio call confirming the passenger ferry has the same setup.

scenes.heading: EXT. ELEVATED ROADWAY, DOWTOWN GOTHAM -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Batman sits on the bat-pod listening and asks Fox about activity on the ferries.

scenes.heading: INT. PASSENGER LOUNGE, COMMUTER FERRY -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The National Guard Commander unwraps a crude remote detonator as the Joker's voice announces over the PA that both ferries are part of a social experiment.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Joker reveals his ferry trap, forcing civilians and prisoners to choose whether to kill each other to survive.

scenes.heading: INT. LAB, WAYNE ENTERPRISES -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Fox's console chimes as he zeros in on the Joker's location while the Joker's voice continues explaining the bomb threat over the ferry PA systems.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROOFTOPS ABOVE GOTHAM -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman fires up the bat-pod and roars into the night toward the Joker's location as Fox guides him west.

scenes.heading: INT. GORDON HOME -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Ramirez calls Barbara Gordon and tells her to evacuate with the kids immediately because the police outside can't be trusted, giving her an address where Jim will meet them.

scenes.heading: EXT. MCU -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Two-Face holds a gun to Ramirez's head as she hangs up the phone, then confronts her about betraying Rachel and flips his coin to decide her fate.

scenes.heading: INT. BRIDGE, PRISONER FERRY -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The pilot of the prisoner ferry discovers the radio is dead as the Joker's voice announces there's no need for everyone to die.

scenes.heading: EXT. PENTHOUSE, PREWITT BUILDING -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Joker stares out at the ferries from a penthouse, talking into a cell phone while holding a detonator with two buttons.

scenes.heading: INT. PASSENGER LOUNGE, PRISONER FERRY -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Joker explains over the PA that each ferry has a remote to blow up the other boat and at midnight he'll blow them both up unless one presses the button first, then hangs up as prisoners begin yelling and the Warden takes the remote.

scenes.heading: EXT. FERRY TERMINAL -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon receives a call from Batman reporting the Joker's location at the Prewitt building.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOTHAM STREETS -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Batman roars along on the bat-pod and tells Gordon to assemble at the building opposite the Prewitt building.

scenes.heading: INT. PASSENGER LOUNGE, COMMUTER FERRY -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: On the commuter ferry, the National Guard Commander holds the remote at gunpoint as passengers debate whether to vote on blowing up the prisoner ferry, with a mother arguing her children shouldn't die for criminals.

scenes.heading: INT. PASSENGER LOUNGE, PRISONER FERRY -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: A corrections officer fires his shotgun into the air to push back angry prisoners on the prisoner ferry.

scenes.heading: INT. PASSENGER LOUNGE, COMMUTER FERRY -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: On the commuter ferry, passengers drop voting chits into a hat and say goodbye to loved ones on their phones as the pilot stares at his blank chit with ten minutes until midnight.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROOFTOP OVERLOOKING PREWITT BUILDING -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gordon and his SWAT team set up sniper and scope positions on a rooftop overlooking the Prewitt Building.

scenes.heading: INT. GARAGE, PREWITT BUILDING -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A SWAT officer reports finding the missing school bus in the Prewitt Building garage.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROOFTOP OVERLOOKING PREWITT BUILDING -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon identifies a hostage situation with the Joker's clown-masked men holding hospital patients, doctors, and nurses at gunpoint in a room with large windows overlooking the ferries.

scenes.heading: EXT. PREWITT BUILDING -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Batman lands against the glass two floors below the hostages and asks Fox for picture.

scenes.heading: INT. LAB, RESEARCH AND DEVELOPMENT -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Fox activates Batman's visual feeds with point-of-view on alpha channel and omni on beta.

scenes.heading: EXT. PREWITT BUILDING -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Smoked glass eyepieces slip down over Batman's cowl and his sonar vision allows him to see through the building's layers to the people inside.

scenes.heading: INT. PENTHOUSE, PREWITT BUILDING -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Joker stands by the penthouse window looking out at the ferries as the Chechen's dogs start barking and he smiles.

scenes.heading: EXT. PENTHOUSE, PREWITT BUILDING -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Batman sprays plastic onto a penthouse window, hardens it, punches through quietly, and slips inside.

scenes.heading: INT. PENTHOUSE, PREWITT BUILDING -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Batman uses sonar vision to detect an armed clown leaning against the corner inside the penthouse.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROOFTOP, PREWITT BUILDING -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A six-man SWAT team prepares to rappel from the rooftop of the Prewitt Building.

scenes.heading: INT. STAIRWELL, PREWITT BUILDING -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A SWAT team moves up the stairwell inside the Prewitt Building.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROOFTOP OVERLOOKING PREWITT BUILDING -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon receives a phone call from Dent who reveals he has Gordon's family at the location where his own family died, then the SWAT leader orders his team to go.
scenes.turningPointNote: Dent reveals he has taken Gordon's family hostage, escalating his revenge plot and splitting the action between two crisis locations

scenes.heading: INT. PENTHOUSE, PREWITT BUILDING -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Batman discovers the armed clowns are actually duct-taped hostages including Engel, while the supposed hostages are the Joker's armed men, as SWAT teams prepare to breach from multiple directions.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Joker's trap is revealed — hostages disguised as gunmen and gunmen disguised as hostages — forcing Batman to fight the SWAT teams to protect innocents

scenes.heading: EXT. ROOFTOP OVERLOOKING PREWITT BUILDING -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: SWAT snipers line up the clowns in their sights from the rooftop.

scenes.heading: INT. STAIRWELL, PREWITT BUILDING -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The SWAT team arrives at the penthouse fire exit and spreads charges across the inner wall.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROOFTOP OVERLOOKING PREWITT BUILDING -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The SWAT leader gives the go order and a sniper watches confused as his target clown disappears.

scenes.heading: INT. PENTHOUSE, PREWITT BUILDING -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Batman yanks a clown along the floor with his grapple gun, taking down others as shots shatter the glass.

scenes.heading: EXT. PREWITT BUILDING -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The SWAT teams rappel down the building and swing in through the broken windows.

scenes.heading: INT. PENTHOUSE, PREWITT BUILDING -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Batman fights the incoming SWAT team with fists and batarangs, then kicks a disguised Joker gunman who was about to shoot a SWAT officer.

scenes.heading: INT. STAIRWELL, PREWITT BUILDING -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: SWAT blasts through the wall and their team leader is yanked screaming through the door by Batman's grapple, then the rest of the team moves in.

scenes.heading: INT. PENTHOUSE, PREWITT BUILDING -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Batman fights the SWAT team while clipping carabiners to their vests, then hurls the team leader out the window causing all of them to be yanked out one by one.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROOFTOP OVERLOOKING PREWITT BUILDING -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A sniper watches the SWAT team hang from the building as Batman secures the line, then Batman fires his grapple gun and yanks the sniper's rifle away.

scenes.heading: INT. PREWITT BUILDING -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Batman races past Engel into the main office where the Joker waits, and the Joker's dogs leap at Batman and smash him to the ground.

scenes.heading: INT. PASSENGER LOUNGE, COMMUTER FERRY -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The pilot of the passenger ferry announces the vote result: 340 for detonating the prisoner ferry, 196 against.

scenes.heading: INT. PASSENGER LOUNGE, PRISONER FERRY -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Corrections officers face off against hundreds of menacing prisoners demanding they detonate the other ferry.

scenes.heading: INT. PENTHOUSE, PREWITT BUILDING -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman wrestles with the Rottweilers and the Joker stabs him in the ribs with a switchblade, then knees and kicks him back towards the glass.

scenes.heading: INT. PASSENGER LOUNGE, COMMUTER FERRY -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The pilot of the passenger ferry sets down the detonator on a bench, refusing to press it himself despite voting for it, and the other passengers stare at it.

scenes.heading: INT. PASSENGER LOUNGE, PRISONER FERRY -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: A huge tattooed prisoner demands the warden give him the detonator, offering to take it by force so the warden can claim innocence.

scenes.heading: INT. PASSENGER LOUNGE, COMMUTER FERRY -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: A businessman picks up the detonator and declares he will press it because the prisoners made their choices and deserve to die.

scenes.heading: INT. PASSENGER LOUNGE, PRISONER FERRY -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The warden hands the prisoner the detonator, but the prisoner tosses it out the window, stunning everyone.
scenes.turningPointNote: The prisoner chooses mercy over survival, defying the Joker's cynical prediction about human nature and removing the threat from one ferry

scenes.heading: EXT. PENTHOUSE, PREWITT BUILDING -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman flies backward through the window and the Joker kicks a steel beam down onto Batman's neck, which Batman barely holds up with his gauntlets.

scenes.heading: INT. PASSENGER LOUNGE, COMMUTER FERRY -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The businessman puts down the detonator without pressing it and sits down to wait as the clock strikes midnight.
scenes.turningPointNote: The civilian ferry also refuses to kill, proving the Joker wrong about humanity and completing the moral victory

scenes.heading: EXT. PENTHOUSE, PREWITT BUILDING -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Batman tells the Joker there will be no fireworks and asks what he was hoping to prove, while the Joker looks at the clock showing midnight.

scenes.heading: INT. BOTH FERRIES -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Passengers on both ferries brace themselves and look at the clock, confused that nothing has happened.

scenes.heading: INT. PREWITT BUILDING -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Batman tells the Joker he's alone, the Joker arms the remote and begins telling how he got his scars, then Batman fires scallop blades into the Joker's chest, kicks him over the edge, grabs the remote, and catches the falling Joker with his grapple.
scenes.turningPointNote: Batman defeats the Joker and stops the detonator, ending the immediate threat but the Joker declares he has won by corrupting Dent

scenes.heading: EXT. BURNT WAREHOUSE, 52ND STREET -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gordon arrives at the burnt warehouse on 52nd Street with his gun drawn and enters the building.

scenes.heading: INT. BURNT WAREHOUSE, 52ND STREET -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gordon calls out for Dent in the darkness and makes his way deeper into the warehouse and up the stairs.

scenes.heading: INT. SECOND FLOOR, BURNT WAREHOUSE -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Gordon finds his family, then Dent cracks him over the head with a gun, disarms him, and explains this is where Rachel died because Gordon made a deal with the devil instead of fighting corruption.

scenes.heading: EXT. BURNT WAREHOUSE, 52ND STREET -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cop cars descend on the warehouse.

scenes.heading: INT. SECOND FLOOR, BURNT WAREHOUSE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Dent holds Gordon's son at gunpoint and declares he will use chance to decide who lives and dies, then flips his coin for Gordon's son, Batman tackles Dent, and both fall over the edge.
scenes.turningPointNote: Dent's fall ends his rampage and forces Batman and Gordon to decide whether to reveal his crimes or protect his legacy

scenes.heading: EXT. BURNT WAREHOUSE, 52ND STREET -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cops prepare to storm the front door of the warehouse.

scenes.heading: INT. BURNT WAREHOUSE, 52ND STREET -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gordon finds Batman injured, they look at Dent's body, and Batman convinces Gordon they must blame Batman for Dent's crimes to preserve hope in Gotham, then Batman flees as Gordon orders the police to chase him.
scenes.turningPointNote: Batman chooses to become a fugitive and sacrifice his reputation to preserve Dent's heroic image and Gotham's hope

scenes.heading: EXT. WAREHOUSE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Police dogs follow Batman's scent away from the warehouse doorway into the shipping containers.

scenes.heading: INT. WAREHOUSE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: James asks his father why they're chasing Batman when he didn't do anything wrong, and Gordon begins to explain.

scenes.heading: EXT. DOCKSIDE ROOFTOPS -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Batman stumbles bleeding to the bat-pod and races through Gotham's streets as Gordon explains in voiceover that Batman is the hero Gotham deserves, a silent guardian and dark knight, then Batman races up a ramp into blinding light.
